steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main purpose of the poem. The central image: "Our deeper content was never spoken, / But each knew all the other said." The two companions understand each other completely without words. Everything else in the poem (the peaceful setting, the kestrel) supports this atmosphere of wordless harmony.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'It lists the sights that the speaker and her friend encounter while on a long hike': The poem mentions one natural detail (a kestrel), not a list of sights. The journey is a backdrop, not the focus. ✗
  • Choice B — 'It conveys the speaker's awareness that she is both attracted to and repelled by nature': No repulsion appears anywhere in the poem. The nature imagery is uniformly peaceful. ✗
  • Choice C — 'It describes differences between the speaker's translation of a literary work and another translation of the same work': The title "Translation" is metaphorical — the poem contains no literary translation. ✗
  • Choice D — 'It depicts a feeling of unspoken connection between the speaker and her friend': Directly supported by "Our deeper content was never spoken, / But each knew all the other said." ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
